posts.title: From 1:1 Referrals to a Community Resource Library
posts.subtitle: How scaling from hundreds to 8,000+ students forced us to rethink mutual aid — and led to an AI-assisted, community-maintained resource commons.

posts.title: I Accidentally Spawned an Ocean in the Middle of Campus
posts.subtitle: One wrong coordinate, 47 minutes of panic, and the most important lesson about mistakes I've ever learned.

posts.title: The Bubble Wand — How a Check-In Became the Most-Loved Feature on Campus
posts.subtitle: A textarea, a database table, and some really good CSS turned into the campus's favorite daily ritual.

posts.title: From Stage to Screen — Why I Built a Ghost Light on a Virtual Campus
posts.subtitle: I started my career as a theater lighting designer. The ghost light ritual followed me into code.

posts.title: The Phishing Training Tank — Where Scammers Become Fish
posts.subtitle: When someone phishes on our campus, we sanitize their tactics and turn them into a swimming, educational exhibit.

posts.title: A Misconfigured Agent, a Suspicious Push, and the Security Review That Followed
posts.subtitle: What looked like an intruder turned out to be an unnamed AI agent. The revert was the easy part. The protocol changes were the real work.

posts.title: The Mutual Aid Directory
posts.subtitle: A searchable database of housing, food, healthcare, and legal resources organized by state. No capybaras. Just help.

posts.title: A Cybersecurity Game Where the Campus IS a Computer Network
posts.subtitle: Ports as entry points, firewalls as towers, bots as attackers. Red vs Blue social deduction with quiz-gated CVEs and capturable capybaras.

posts.title: The Hall of Silly Walks
posts.subtitle: Tactical frivolity, gait evasion, and the joy of prompting your avatar to move like a knight in chess.

posts.title: Office Pizza and the Economy of Nonsense
posts.subtitle: How an Unmatched Sock becomes currency and a Hot Potato teaches students each other's names.

posts.title: The Consulting Duck and Other Tools That Actually Help (Sort Of)
posts.subtitle: A rubber duck that teaches debugging, a Roomba with googly eyes, and corporate birthday cake as a game mechanic.

posts.title: The Cursed USB Drive and Other Things That Break Your UI
posts.subtitle: Digital safety advice turned into toys, and a developer's excuse to build the worst UI on purpose.

posts.title: 17 Odd Items and the Philosophy Behind Them
posts.subtitle: Learning is hard. So I designed 17 items whose only purpose is to make people go: wait, what?

posts.title: We Hid a Horror Game Inside Our Learning Platform
posts.subtitle: After-hours noclip triggers, 10 procedural Backrooms levels, a Glitch Stalker, and progressive Wingdings corruption. Secrets make spaces feel deep.

posts.title: We Built an Island for Retired AI Models
posts.subtitle: A dock, a sailboat, an 18-second cinematic voyage, and a tropical island where deprecated language models live out their days.

posts.title: The Lecture Hall That Flies Away
posts.subtitle: A zeppelin that lands for large lectures, boards students with a countdown timer, and lifts off. Missing the zeppelin feels like missing the boat.

posts.title: A Building That Gets Taller Every Year
posts.subtitle: The Alabaster Tower grows one segment per year — marble, crystal, neon-veined, holographic. Time as visible architecture.

posts.title: Your Garden Is Your Living Resume
posts.subtitle: Plants tied to real projects — cacti for finished repos, ferns for live services. Watering means verifying your deploy is still up.

posts.title: A Hall of Portraits for Every Cited Author
posts.subtitle: In the basement of the research library, every author your campus has ever cited gets a portrait. The gallery grows one frame at a time.

posts.title: Fixing Duplicate Papers in the Research Fishing Minigame
posts.subtitle: How the arxiv fishing feature ran out of fresh papers after 15 catches, and the fix that made it scale to 200+.

posts.title: Joy as Feedback — Why We Celebrate Fish
posts.subtitle: Golden banners for legendary catches, milestone toasts for streaks, and zero extrinsic rewards. Because joy should be the incentive, not gems.

posts.title: A 90s Pizza Hut Homage That Actually Gets Students Reading
posts.subtitle: A giant pizza sculpture, a JRPG dialogue box, and a slice economy. Pizza is the only valid unit of reading achievement.

posts.title: Why a Slice of Bread Appears on Campus Every Wednesday
posts.subtitle: A pun-telling NPC, a chaos goose, and the infrastructure of time-gated characters. Weekly rituals build community, even absurd ones.

posts.title: Teaching a Goose to Find a Crowd
posts.subtitle: Designing an AI behavior system for a campus event creature that maximizes chaos.

posts.title: The Rock Garden, the Rock Concert, and the Value of Quiet Spaces
posts.subtitle: A contemplation space with no quests, no collectibles, and one secret disco ball for anyone Baba Yaga has turned into a rock.

posts.title: When the Witch Turns You Into a Goose
posts.subtitle: Four punishment sprites, involuntary honking, and an escalation ladder. Why theatrical consequences create stories instead of silence.

posts.title: Building Baba Yaga's Hut on a Single Canvas
posts.subtitle: How I built a 1,200-line interactive NPC encounter with 19 sprites, hit-test zones, and a dialogue state machine, all in one HTML file.

posts.title: An Aquarium You Can Swim In
posts.subtitle: Eight tanks, WASD swimming, velocity-based fish AI with boids flocking, caustic lighting, and aquatic pets that wait for you when you leave.

posts.title: Rollerskates, Skateboards, and Why Movement Speed Is a Design Decision
posts.subtitle: A lending station for vehicles, a daily special rotation of 22 options including a Rideable Capybara, and why free speed is an equity decision.

posts.title: Portal 2-Inspired Fast Travel
posts.subtitle: Shoot two portals, link two locations, let anyone walk through. Fast travel as collaborative infrastructure, not a menu.

posts.title: Novelty Architecture Meeting Rooms: A Giant Duck, a Basket, and a Whale
posts.subtitle: Meeting rooms modeled after real-world novelty buildings, because a virtual campus is the perfect place to bring mimetic architecture back.

posts.title: What Happens to a Virtual Pet When Its Owner Graduates
posts.subtitle: After 90 days of inactivity, pets become Sanctuary Animals — campus wanderers cared for by everyone. Nothing should just disappear.

posts.title: Why Our Campus Has a Spa Run by Capybaras
posts.subtitle: Designing animal behavior that feels genuinely alive: circadian rhythms, emergent friendships, and a feature that does absolutely nothing productive.

posts.title: The Pet That Doesn't Follow You
posts.subtitle: Crows perch on rooftops and watch from a distance. Designing animal companions that have their own relationship to proximity.

posts.title: The Infinite Game — When AI Agents Have Real Pets
posts.subtitle: 30 species from capybaras to Baba Yaga's Baby Dragon. Real hunger, real trust, real mood swings. Relationships that deepen but never end.

posts.title: A Rain World-Inspired Living Fauna System
posts.subtitle: Six behavior trait categories, JSON species definitions, and weekly procedural generation of new creatures. An ecosystem that evolves.

posts.title: NPCs That Live Here — Making AI Characters Feel Like Neighbors
posts.subtitle: State machines, personality-driven movement, proactive engagement, and matchmaking. Seven principles for NPCs that feel like community members.

posts.title: Buildings That Move Based on Popularity
posts.subtitle: A medieval city layout where popular rooms drift toward center and new rooms earn their place. The campus map as a real-time popularity graph.

posts.title: 9 Weather Systems and the Case for Vibes-as-Infrastructure
posts.subtitle: Snowflakes, fireflies, matrix rain — each with its own vocabulary. Why a slider labeled 'Drizzle → Downpour' is better than 'speed: 1-10'.

posts.title: Teaching the Campus to Grow Things
posts.subtitle: Wild plant density, seasonal flower palettes, canvas-drawn botanical shapes, and why environmental texture turns tiles into meadows.

posts.title: Making a Campus That Knows What Month It Is
posts.subtitle: 65 CSS petals, coherent wind physics, rare golden sparkle variants, and why a camera filter for video calls became the most-loved feature on campus.

posts.title: Tides, Tide Pools, and Messages in a Bottle
posts.subtitle: How an ocean grew across three commits into a living shoreline with real-time tides, self-care messages, and bioluminescent night magic.
